comment: Live Claude Coach rule file. The weekly loop proposes additions/retirements; a human approves. kinds: regex (on prompt), context_threshold (est. context tokens), prompt_length (chars), capability_gap (regex on prompt + feature metadata: feature_id, doc_url, category), tool_sequence (regex over recent tool tokens - Bash rendered as Bash:<cmd>, other tools as their plain name - + feature metadata: feature_id, doc_url, category).

rules.pattern: ^(please )?(commit( (and|&))? push|push( it)?( up)?|continue|go on|keep going|next( step)?|proceed)\.?$|^what('s| is) (the )?next( step| task)?\??$|^do you know what (the |our )?next task is\??$
rules.message: ⚠ Coach: babysitting prompt #{count} this week. This 3-word turn re-reads the ENTIRE session context. Batch instructions up front, or make it a CLAUDE.md standing rule (e.g. 'commit and push after each module without asking').

⚠ Coach: this session is at ~{ctx_k}k context tokens. Every turn from here costs 3x+ baseline. At the next natural pause: run the handoff-after-clear skill, then /clear. (Audit: sessions like this one were ~49% of all historical spend.)

🔴 Coach ESCALATION: ~{ctx_k}k context tokens. This is god-session territory - the single most expensive habit on record. Stop adding scope. Run handoff-after-clear NOW, commit state to HANDOFF.md, then /clear. A fresh session resumes from the file at ~15x lower cost per turn.
⚠ Coach: this session created a task list but never updated it — a dead task list burns tokens with zero tracking value. Either keep it current or skip TaskCreate for work this size.
⚠ Coach: this prompt is over 8k characters. Pasted content stays in context EVERY turn for the rest of the session. If it lives in a file, reference the path instead and let Claude read selectively.
